name: Zerodha Trading Platform — Eureka Integrated (No Hardcoded URLs)
scenario: Remove all hardcoded URLs from the Zerodha trading platform. Add Eureka service discovery: a eureka-server, and register both market-service and order-service. Feign clients find services by name. Run two market-service instances to observe load balancing.
description: Three-service platform: eureka-server (registry) + market-service (producer) + order-service (consumer with Feign). All services registered with Eureka. No url= in any @FeignClient. Load balancing demonstrated with two market-service instances.

core.description: `eureka-server` Spring Boot app: @EnableEurekaServer, port=8761, register-with-eureka=false, fetch-registry=false, self-preservation=false. Dashboard accessible at http://localhost:8761.

`market-service` with @EnableDiscoveryClient, spring.application.name=market-service, eureka.client.service-url.defaultZone configured. Endpoint: GET /api/stocks/{symbol}/quote returns {symbol, price, tradeable, handledByPort}.
`order-service` with @EnableDiscoveryClient + @EnableFeignClients. `MarketServiceClient` @FeignClient(name='market-service') — NO url= attribute. spring-cloud-starter-loadbalancer in pom.xml.

`TradeService.executeTrade(userId, symbol, qty)` uses MarketServiceClient (Feign, discovered via Eureka). Validates tradeable flag. Returns TradeConfirmation. Records which market-service port handled the request.
@SpringBootTest test for TradeService with Eureka disabled (eureka.client.enabled=false) and @MockBean MarketServiceClient. Tests: success, not tradeable (throws). Both tests pass.
Show load balancing: start two market-service instances (ports 8081 + 8082), place 6 trades via order-service, verify logs show alternating ports (round-robin). Document in README.

bonus.description: Add custom metadata to market-service: eureka.instance.metadata-map.version=1.0 and metadata-map.region=ap-south-1. Write RegistryController in order-service (GET /admin/registry) that shows all services with their metadata.

Add a third service: notification-service (port 8083) that registers with Eureka. Add NotificationServiceClient @FeignClient to order-service. After a trade executes, call notification-service (async) to send a confirmation.

Add Actuator health integration: eureka.client.healthcheck.enabled=true. Write a custom OrderDbHealthIndicator. Simulate DB going DOWN and verify market-service stops routing to the unhealthy order-service instance.

data_to_use:
stock quotes:
TCS:     price=3890, tradeable=true
RELIANCE: price=2450, tradeable=true
INFY:    price=1560, tradeable=true
PAYTM:   price=475,  tradeable=false

Load balancing test:
Start market-service on :8081 and :8082
Place 6 trades → expect alternating: 8081, 8082, 8081, 8082, 8081, 8082
example_output:
# Start sequence:
$ java -jar eureka-server.jar          # http://localhost:8761
$ java -jar market-service.jar --server.port=8081
$ java -jar market-service.jar --server.port=8082
$ java -jar order-service.jar          # port 8082

# Eureka dashboard shows:
# MARKET-SERVICE  →  2 instances (8081, 8082)
# ORDER-SERVICE   →  1 instance (8082)

# Place 6 trades:
curl -X POST http://localhost:8082/api/trades -d '{"symbol":"TCS","qty":10}'

# order-service log:
# Trade handled by market-service on port: 8081
# Trade handled by market-service on port: 8082
# Trade handled by market-service on port: 8081
# Trade handled by market-service on port: 8082
# Trade handled by market-service on port: 8081
# Trade handled by market-service on port: 8082

# Tests:
✓ executeTrade_TCS_success
✓ executeTrade_PAYTM_notTradeable
ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. Critical: remove url= from @FeignClient and add spring-cloud-starter-loadbalancer dependency — both needed. eureka.instance.instance-id=${spring.application.name}:${server.port} prevents ID collision when running 2 instances. Test: @TestPropertySource(properties='eureka.client.enabled=false') + @MockBean FeignInterface.
submission_instructions: Three separate GitHub repos (or subdirectories). README shows startup order and load balancing log proof. Both @SpringBootTest tests pass.
tips_for_success: Start order: eureka-server first, wait for 'Started' log, then services
LoadBalancer: spring-cloud-starter-loadbalancer in order-service pom — REQUIRED for url-less Feign
Load balancing: the /api/instance endpoint returns server.port — log it in each trade
Test: eureka.client.enabled=false prevents test from trying to connect to Eureka
concepts_reinforced: Round-robin load balancing across multiple instances
Disabling Eureka in tests with eureka.client.enabled=false
